response.justification: The candidate has tangential geospatial exposure through the GISCollective open-source project and Green Map GIS work, but the role's primary technical requirement — commercial-grade C++ or Rust development — is nearly absent from their profile. Their only C/C++ work is a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a student thesis project, both dated and limited in scope. The 15+ year career is overwhelmingly TypeScript, Node.js, and web-focused. There is no evidence of routing data pipelines, navigation tile generation, or production-grade geospatial systems engineering at the scale this role demands.
strengths: Geospatial domain exposure via GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform used in production by NGOs) and Green Map GIS application
High-throughput distributed systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency DLang applications
Polyglot background across DLang, C#, Java, and C++ demonstrates willingness to expand beyond current expertise
concerns: C++ experience is limited to a 6-month role in 2014–2015 and a student thesis — far below the commercial C++ depth required
No Rust experience listed anywhere in the resume
Geospatial work is hobbyist/NGO-grade open-source mapping, not production routing tile pipelines or navigation data systems

key_factors.factor: No routing or navigation pipeline experience
